channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: /smg/ — 345 replies — 1380.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
/smg/

Open thread • Replies: 345 • Updated: 2026-05-14 14:40 UTC

OP

Real world edition

>Educational sites:
https://www.investopedia.com

>Financial TV Streams:
https://www.newslive.com/american/cnbc.html

https://www.tradingview.com
https://www.finscreener.com
https://www.portfoliovisualizer.com

>Screeners:
https://finviz.com/
https://etfdb.com/

>Gambling:
https://www.optionsplaybook.com/options-introduction/
https://www.optionsprofitcalculator.com
https://optionstrat.com/
https://www.optionistics.com/quotes/option-prices

>Pre-Market and Live data:
https://www.investing.com/indices/indices-futures
https://finance.yahoo.com/

>Calendars
https://www.marketwatch.com/economy-politics/calendar
https://www.earningswhispers.com/calendar
https://www.cmegroup.com/trading/interest-rates/countdown-to-fomc.html
https://www.mortgagenewsdaily.com/data/building-permits

>Boomer Investing 101:
https://www.bogleheads.org/wiki/Getting_started
https://www.sec.gov/search-filings

>Misc smg:
https://www.financialjuice.com/home
https://finance.yahoo.com/trending-tickers
https://market24hclock.com/
https://www.dividendchannel.com/drip-returns-calculator
https://brokerchooser.com/
https://www.chathamfinancial.com/technology/us-market-rates

62230412

62230413
What’s the deal with silver anyway?

62230415

Damn it's near 3 year lows

62230419
>>62230396
I didn't imply QCOM isn't a decent long, you absolute imbecile. I said if semis as a sector and tech as a whole go down, then QCOM is the best short of them all as an insurance play, because they are tied to a big consumer sector rather than the assured capex of megacorporations.

62230422
At least NVDA is holding up my gains today.

62230424

>The new US fed chair no longer wants to publicly signal changes to the interest rate in advance
>He wants to move towards a range rather than 2% inflation target so you can't even model Fed moves

So I guess it was getting too difficult for insiders to make more money?

62230429
>>62230422
I'm still mildly in the green thanks to my QQQ plays.

62230432

That's what I'm doing. QCOM is one of the first legs to fall on semis, and it's doing it already. Already down 5% today

62230440

62230442

62230443

62230444

62230446

62230449
Week 4 of being overbought

item.title: You should be holding at least 1 BTC. — 62 replies — 41.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
You should be holding at least 1 BTC.

Open thread • Replies: 62 • Updated: 2026-05-14 13:11 UTC

OP

Imagine not owning at least 1 BTC before institutions and governments vacuum the remaining supply while you’re still debating if Bitcoin is a scam… or just trading crypto without considering BTC long term.

Early replies (first 3)

62219945
>>62219942
Or I can trade alts (leveraged BTC) and actually make it instead of getting a 2x pump

62219995
Fuck. I have 2BTC . Is that enough bro?

62220027
>>62219995
no, i have 3. and it's not enough, we're just poor faggots

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62228616
>>62225050
you're poor if you're only earning $5 per hour

62228631
>>62222594
>wantcum cumpooping
what

62228635
>>62226434
No, i was being a dick and suggesting that some things can just go downwards

62228803
>>62220146
Yeah probably if you think long term and keep self custody. You not considering staking part of it on Babylon though instead of letting all 5 BTC sit there forever?

62228807
>>62228635
I'm just being sarcastic though

62228809
>>62225050
Just keep working hard and you're gonna make it anon

62228821
>>62228803
No way in hell am I gonna risk my bitcoin for some pennies on some centralized exchange.

62228833
Btc is Epstein pedo coin…it’s all a con, they were pissing their pants over xrp

God coin = XRP

62228837
>>62228833

62228839
>>62228821
too risky

62228849
>>62228809

62229042
>>62228821

62229048
>>62222489
Why LTC?

62229075
>>62228833
God coin? wake up

62229077
>>62228839
Every platform has risks

62229086
>>62225044
Wait for $1M and have a good life for the rest of your life

62229114
>>62229042
I don't trust "smart" contracts either for holding substantial funds long term. The most I've ever been willing to risk is like $50k on DeFi.

62229336
>>62229114
I know anon, but the newer native BTC infrastructure is built more around predefined protocol rules while still keeping self custody. And nobody’s saying you need to ape your entire stack into it either.

62229340
>>62228391
Sure, poorfag
>>62229086

62229342
>>62220158

62229433
>>62229340
Pass it to your children. Hodl

62229475
>>62219942

62229508

62229551
>>62229433
I don't have kids, it's over
>>62229508

62229557
>>62229114

>>62229551

62229561
>>62229551
What about forex?

62229570
>>62229557
Yeah, don't think I'm getting kids anytime soon and blew it with one of the few women I might had.
>>62229561
I'm an europoor and i don't do forex trading. I just hold and dca my wagie paycheck, but BTC has been shit this whole market run. And yes the opportunity cost once again is making no sense. Why not pile everything into boomer stocks, better risk and reward after all. I might need to diversify my bags and forget about Bitcoin for a while.
Godspeed

62229578
>>62229570
Be loyal ser and start a good family

62230004

62230021
>>62219942

item.title: THE STOCK MARKET KEEPS PUMPING — 6 replies — 23.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
THE STOCK MARKET KEEPS PUMPING

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-05-14 14:25 UTC

OP

Because of Trump and Xis friendship
AMERICA IS SO BACK

Early replies (first 3)

62229248
Where's Epstein in this picture?

62229260
For decades, pharmacies were a key part of American life, serving as places where people could pick up prescriptions, groceries, toiletries, and everyday essentials. Today, that system is rapidly declining. Thousands of pharmacies across the United States have closed, leaving many communities with fewer healthcare options and creating “pharmacy deserts,” especially in rural areas where the nearest pharmacy may be over an hour away.

Chains like CVS and Walgreens once expanded aggressively and dominated the market, generating billions in prescription revenue. However, changing shopping habits have weakened their business. Many customers now buy household products from Amazon or Walmart, where prices are often lower. Drugstores also frustrated shoppers by locking up common items like toothpaste and deodorant due to theft concerns, making shopping less convenient.

At the same time, pharmacies face staffing shortages, employee burnout, rising operating costs, and shrinking profits. Pharmacists and store workers report overwhelming workloads and understaffed locations, leading to long wait times and declining customer service.

Another major issue is the growing influence of pharmacy benefit managers, or PBMs. These middlemen control drug pricing and reimbursements between insurers, manufacturers, and pharmacies. Critics argue PBMs reduce pharmacy profits while increasing costs for consumers, making it difficult for both major chains and independent pharmacies to survive.

Rite Aid filed for bankruptcy, Walgreens reported massive losses, and even CVS has shifted focus toward healthcare services and insurance rather than traditional retail pharmacy. Experts warn that continued closures could seriously reduce access to medications and healthcare, especially for low-income and rural Americans.

62229265
>>62229238
This shit sucks without Epstein

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62230023
>>62229265

62230148
>>62229260
thanks chatgpt, sounds like it's fucking over

62230378
>>62230023
the boys are back
the boys are back
the boys are back in tow~n

item.title: >see that Normiebase is having a promotion for its "predictions market" >might be cool, can win 5 BTC >start to enter pr… — 4 replies — 16.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>see that Normiebase is having a promotion for its "predictions market" >might be cool, can win 5 BTC >start to enter pr…

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-05-14 14:40 UTC

OP

>see that Normiebase is having a promotion for its "predictions market"
>might be cool, can win 5 BTC
>start to enter predictions
>"Please enter your Employer, Salary, Full Name, Address, Social Security Number, blood type, credit card number, mother's maiden name"
>exit app, turn 360 degrees and moonwalk away

For what purpose? Do normies really give away all of their personal information to retarded companies like normiebase?

Early replies (first 3)

62230403
>>62230387
Ok terrorist

62230423
There's nothing to be afraid of if you have nothing to hide.

62230426
i never won these coinbase events so i stopped participating

really a jeet exchange nowadays

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62230450
>>62230403
>>62230423
Well besides the fact that's a retarded mentality, this >>62230426 is the whole point, there's no guarantee that I'd even get anything. In fact I most likely won't. So why give them all this personal info in exchange for nothing at all?

item.title: how to save ICP? — 4 replies — 16.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
how to save ICP?

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-05-14 14:32 UTC

OP

for more context, i'm a solo motoko dev. i want more eyes on ICP so our bags can pump.

i'm thinking a canister that can host censorable thing and will make a lot of people mad & outraged. i plan to share the canister on /pol/, but what should i host? JQ is mainstream now so i doubt anyone would care.

or do you anons have any other idea? anyway, i'm just tired of holding for 5 years. my final option is to wait for toko to launch then do an nft collection there... ugh...

Early replies (first 3)

62230305
>>62230261
Badlands, GPU Subnets, a PoW shitcoin (like BOB) that actually moons and doesnt dump below 100m mcap, blacklisting the entire third world.

Too bad none of these will ever happen

62230390
>>62230305
>PoW
what's stopping anyone from creating a new pow coin on icp?

62230410
>>62230261
Maybe not make it an uncapped VC scam token? Idk might be too much to ask of Dominic. Also raj is a scammer fuck icp

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62230416
>>62230261
There is no "censored-thing hosting" on ICP, you can't even become a host without approval. It will be removed immediately.

item.title: WATCH CLOSELY - APRIL, MAY, JUNE, JULY - WATCH CLOSELY STAY ALERT - EARLY WARNING SYSTEMS WILL FAIL - DOUBLE MEANING RES… — 3 replies — 12.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
WATCH CLOSELY - APRIL, MAY, JUNE, JULY - WATCH CLOSELY STAY ALERT - EARLY WARNING SYSTEMS WILL FAIL - DOUBLE MEANING RES…

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-05-14 14:30 UTC

OP

WATCH CLOSELY - APRIL, MAY, JUNE, JULY - WATCH CLOSELY

STAY ALERT - EARLY WARNING SYSTEMS WILL FAIL - DOUBLE MEANING

RESET WILL OCCUR SWIFTLY - FINANCIAL RESET - OVERNIGHT

SWIFT TO BE 'READJUSTED 2021 - 2022' - ABRUPT CHANGE

MULTIPLE DIGITAL ASSETS TO BE INTEGRATED INTO THE FUTURE FINANCIAL SYSTEMS

UBI - ASSISTANCE FOR THE PEOPLE - STIMULATION FOR THE ECONOMY - REAL MOTIVES HIDDEN

DO NOT LOOK FURTHER - ALL ANSWERS - IN THE PAST - FUTURE WILL BE REPEATED - THIS IS A SIMULATION ATTEMPT - YOU CANNOT STOP IT

BE AWARE OF SUDDEN 'EVENTS' - UNE.X.PECTED - WORLD SHATTERING

SEEK SAFETY IN THE EVENT OF 311 - 911 - 1131 - INTE.R.NAL - http://SPREA.D.ING WORLDWIDE

FEAR EQUALS .C.ONTROL

NEW WORLD AWAITS - ONLY FEW WILL BE A PART OF IT - SIMULATIONS WILL CONTINUE

SEEK WISDOMS IN BOOKS

BEWARE OF SUDDEN DEVALUATION OF FIAT - BANKS NOT SAFE - RESET - RESTRUCTURING

SOON AN OP.P.ORTUNITY WILL COME TO 'REINVEST' - DO IT - DO NOT WAIT

DREAMS WILL SOON BECOME A REALITY FOR SOME AND YET MANY WILL STILL MISS OUT

FOLLOW THE BLUE LIGHT - IT WILL LEAD YOU TO A AVALANCHE

FOLLOW THE GREEN COLORS - THEY WILL LEAD YOU TO PROSPERITY

NOT MUCH TIME IS LEFT - WORLD AS YOU KNOW IT IS ABOUT TO CHANGE FOR EVER

LOOK CLOSER - MORE INFORMATION IS HELD RIGHT IN FRONT OF YOU - HERE - HERE - HERE

THE POOLS OF LIQUIDITY ARE ABOUT TO OPEN UP FOR THE PUBLIC EYES - PATIENCE IS REQUIRED - ONLY FEW WILL REMAIN IN CONTROL

ARE YOU READY FOR WHAT'S TO COME - YOU HAVEN'T SEEN ANYTHING YET - 2020 WAS A TEST, ONE OF MANY

SCOTUS

TIME HAS A COMING

SWIFT - LIVE - ANNOUNCEMENT - FOLLOW RAGNAROK

THE PATTERN WILL EMERGE
THE PATTERN WILL EMERGE
THE PATTERN WILL EMERGE

62230067

62230409
the thought of everything happening in one night is comfy over the idea that they'll slowly grind your soul over decades of waiting BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

item.title: This is getting ridiculous — 28 replies — 15.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
This is getting ridiculous

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-05-14 12:54 UTC

OP

MSFT is currently trading at January 2024 prices. That’s 2.5 years of zero gains. Meanwhile the company is one of the largest and best in the world and growing in the high teens every year. I can’t see how this isn’t a screaming buy right now. I recognize the uncertainly surrounding their investment into AI, and possibly the negative sentiment around software stocks in general but for me Microsoft isn’t going anywhere

Early replies (first 3)

62228288
>>62228282
i bought around $400. i also bought META yesterday.

MA and V are good buys now also.

62228292
>>62228282
I have an average price per share of $366 I just keep buying along with Amazon Google and Apple.

62228297
>>62228288
V is a screaming buy but I don’t know enough about stable coins to assess the proper risk to the credit card business yet.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62228304
>>62228282
when it dropped to like $360 or whatever, i still didn't buy this piece of shit

62228309
>>62228282
your money could've been making more money buying mu and sndk at the top than holding this crap

62228317
>>62228282
openAI is a pile of shit

62228321
>>62228309
Yessir

62228343
windows is the boomer os thats being jeeted to hell, and everyone can see the writing on the wall. its like everytime a new windows version drops the screams of dissatisfaction get louder. Thats what happens after 20 years of letting indians nepo hire more of their kind. everyone is leaving azure for AWS too. gen alpha tr00ns will all be vibecoding on mac minis and lenovos running fedora. think of how many PowerBI boomers are retiring in the next decade. why even bother with x86 when the day of the pillow finally arrives and frees us all.

62228415
>>62228297
A company trading at a PE of around 28 is screaming buy? It's only down like 10% in the last year.

62228438
>>62228282
The only thing Microsoft does well is datacenter hosting. Everything else they do is shit.

They make their gains through shady business practices like dark patterns and built in spyware or buying out and shutting down the competition. Not by having a best in class product and marketing it well.

You need to take all of those things into consideration before going long on MSFT.

62228448
Reason the share price is stagnant is cuz all of the people laid off there got their RSUs early and have cashed out.

62228452
The market (chiefly AI) is fully saturated with all the monopoly money anyone is currently willing to risk. Things have to start dying for other things to achieve "fair value"

62228457
>>62228282
Culmination of Azure getting mogged by AWS and ChatGPT (Microsoft is balls deep in OpenAI) losing ground to Gemini and Claude.

62228475
>>62228415
They are a digital payment toll keeper and the bridge is already built a small amount of maintenance capital expenditure per year and you are set.

62228486
>>62228282
If AI fails to boost economic growth as much as the markets expect then tech stocks crash but if it does then software loses value.
MSFT might be worth buying starting at around 200-250. Currently indexes are overheated on the HTFs which makes long-term investments risky.

62228488
>>62228415
one day /biz/ will learn to look at the 1 year forward PE and growth rate

62228489
>>62228486
its already boosting economic growth

62228502
>>62228282
>Meanwhile the company is one of the largest and best in the world
their main product is worthless
you don't need any office products, you can interact with them online/ through a FOSS or through your agent/LLM
you also don't make them yourself anymore

microsaar has been dumping money into openAI but they will not be getting any equity
they're on track to become IBM 2

62228524
>>62228489
Real GDP YoY isn't spiking at all. Should be like 5% to justify the parabolic market rally.

62228537
>>62228282
>5% linkedin layoffs
>20-30% of some teams wiped company wide
>ticker didn't even twitch
lmao macroshart

62228596
Designated shitting company

62228906
Microsoft is history. We are going to see at least 80%-90% downside from here. Nothing is going to save this.

62228910
Also notice Nadella wasn’t invited to China. He’s the biggest embarrassment in the tech sector.

62228937
>>62228282
idk pal, if that was a coin would you buy this chart?

62229462
>>62228282
So well positioned after turning their OS into a Linux bootloader with extra spyware and renaming their flagship Office products after a shit chatbot and making them rolling updates so no longer stable either as UI or backwards compatibility.
Do they still do the Xbox thing?
Only positive I can find is that the pivot to AI will optimize their enshittification workload and remove billions of jeets but I don't know how that can be monetized.

62229815
>>62228282
It will inevitably swing upwards unless the AI bubble collapses, but in that case you wouldn't want to hold any other stocks anyway.
Or you could just put everything in Nebius, Sivers and Silicon Motion if you're convinced the AI boom will persist.

62229845
>>62228910
Wasn't he called up to testify in the OpenAI lawsuit the other day?
He probably doesn't appreciate missing out on that while Elon schmoozes with Xi and Trump.

62229958
>>62228282
this company is completely jeeted and its only a matter of time when people stop trusting it, other companies need security most and trusting jeets with security is a big gamble, my prediction is that many companies working with MS will be looking for alternatives.

62229410
>>62229123

62229519
>>62229410

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

Ripple is a scam. The clarity act is anti cripto. Cripto is anti government. Fuck off you tourist.

62229527

62229552
PLEASE BUY MY BAGS .. PLEASE BUY MY BAGS ..... PLEASE BUY MY BAGS ... PLEASE BUY MY BAGS ...PLEASE BUY MY BAGS

62229758

62229861
>>62229758
bullish

62230108

62230120
>>62229123

62230226
>>62229123

You have been warned.

item.title: Bitcoin or altcoins? — 92 replies — 10.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Bitcoin or altcoins?

Open thread • Replies: 92 • Updated: 2026-05-14 05:43 UTC

OP

If you had to pick, what would you buy right now: BTC or altcoins? I’m not seeing much ROI in alts lately, even utility or memecoins.

Early replies (first 3)

62195293
>>62195275
Bitcoin until 120k

62195300
you can't time the market I thought like >>62195293
last time, bitcoin to 70k and sell half and go into alts and then 2021 alts just fucking died for a year.

The alts that pumped are things that haven't gone parabolic yet, or big projects like Solana with glowie money attached to them. Ones like Link are not going to 1000 regardless of cope.

/biz/ will pretty much only give you bad info too. I've found maybe 3 coins here that were Guaranteed returns, everything else I had to research myself. In fact I remember last year posting proof that CRCL stock was going to 10x on IPO and got XRP schizos fudding me out of taking a life changing position.

I actually hate the fuck out of baggies.

62195373
>>62195293
Why 120k only?? Why not 1M?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62209376
>>62207965
Let's see how it goes

62209379
>>62204588
So are you into btc?

62209385
Bitcoin is getting fucked

62209525
>>62209385
Utility coins barely move unless the market gets euphoric again. Just btc

62209590
People pretending fundamentals matter in crypto will never stop being funny.

62209854
>>62195275
Everyone becomes a macro analyst after holding one red candle lmao

62210047
>>62209304
but when?

62210050
>>62209291
hold and stake

62211194
>>62207855
I've got some hbar in my portfolio

62211429
>>62211194
hbar going down to 2c before it ever gets a chance at real traction

62211440
>>62195275
>Chainlink is the future

62211870
>>62211440
Hahaha

62211903
>>62195275
Everyone moved to BTC of course, I dumped my ETH for BTC too, I used to hold BTC because of staking but now I can stake BTC natively thanks to TBV and the likes

62211915
>>62195275
I’d rather keep stacking BTC, then put it to work via native staking / yield layers (like Babylon-style setups) instead of chasing dead charts and hoping for a narrative pump.

62211928
>>62209385
Ok as you say so

62211932
>>62211429
Hbar is okay to hold and buy

62212034
>>62195275
More potential upside on alts for the next six months. Specifically memes right now.

62213245
>>62211440
What's your price target anon?

62214696
>>62211932
All in or?

62216973
>>62211915
It's worth considering anon

62216987
>>62195275

Polkadot

62220595
>>62209854
Are you sure?

62224657
>>62216987
Why polkadot?

62224666
cny/usd is > .147 now w/trump headed to china. every penny that can chase risk on is gonna get horned up immediately. The ONLY thing the US has left is the dollar as a w3@p0n. Plus, i can't afford any more makers mark bottles unless my alts start pumping w/haste, so...

62224894
>>62224666
Crazy

62224972
>>62224666
We're cooked

62226444
>>62224666
Risk-on driven by geopolitics, liquidity, and alcoholism. Peak crypto posting.

62228393
>>62216987
>ICP

62228841
chainlink

62229090
>>62216987
he's also pedo

item.title: NEW PARADIGM — 15 replies — 7.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
NEW PARADIGM

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-05-14 12:42 UTC

OP

Everyone is going to be a multimillionaire!

Early replies (first 3)

62229473
>>62229457
Only if you had a million already invested

62229483
Now post the log chart, also inflation adjusted.

62229495
Look up "SPX/USM2" on tradingview. Zoom out.

You can also do SPX/USGDP

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62229496
>>62229457
My hairdresser in bottomtober.

62229516
>>62229457
Well yes, but a million are what 10000 used to be. Do i have to explain to you the concept of Inflation?

62229560
>>62229483
If you do that, its at the same level as the dot com bubble. How does thst support your assumption that its fine?

62229669
>>62229560
Official Inflation is systematically too low. Its likely 40% below what you think with that in mind.

62229675
>>62229473
Not enough, for an all around comfy life you need double digit millions these days.

62229696
>>62229675
So untrue I want to slap you and take all your food.

62229737
>>62229696
Comfy house in the Alps will cost you a million +. Of course, if you're able to live a comfy life in a commie block or favela then merely single digit million will suffice.

62229788
>>62229737
I know your standard for an all around comfy life is absurd. That's why I'm slapping you and taking away your food privileges.

62229804
>>62229457
>Everyone is going to be a multimillionaire

With inflation, probably.

62229808
>>62229788
Having a house in a first world high trust nation as a gtfo option isn't absurd lol.

62229843
>>62229457
Covid was nothing on this chart. Bidenvid in ‘22 was worse than the Dot Com crash but the media was silent about it so it didn’t happen.

62229923
>>62229843
The market dumped because they raised rates to fight inflation.
The inflation was caused by pic related and Biden’s additional stimulus. In the end, Trump printed more.

Sorry migger
